hero.badge: Faculty of Science and Technology · PSU Pattani
hero.desc_1: Supporting mathematics education and research through the collaboration between Prince of Songkla University and the TUYF Charitable Trust Fund.

about.title: Projects Supported by the PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund
about.desc_1: Since 2021, the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus, has been honored to receive significant support from the PSU-TUYF project — a collaboration between Prince of Songkla University and the TUYF Charitable Trust Fund.
about.desc_2: These initiatives are dedicated to elevating the quality of education and research in mathematics. The department currently receives funding for three main projects.

programs.title: Three Main Projects under PSU-TUYF
programs.subtitle: Projects dedicated to elevating the quality of education and research in mathematics.

mscd.title: Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development
mscd.desc: Developing mathematical potential at all levels — students, undergraduates, and teachers — to build a foundation for sustainable community development.

mgss.title: Mathematical Graduated Students Supporting Project
mgss.desc: Promoting research and teaching, with funding to support highly capable students in graduate-level studies.

algebra.title: Algebra Enrichment Project
algebra.desc: Enhancing algebra knowledge in Thailand and establishing a southern Algebra Center as a hub for learning and research.

mscd.title: About the Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development Project
mscd.sub: โครงการพัฒนาชุมชนอย่างยั่งยืนด้วยคณิตศาสตร์
mscd.desc: This project focuses on developing mathematical potential at various levels, including secondary school students, undergraduate students, and mathematics teachers in secondary schools. Its goal is to strengthen the foundational knowledge and essential skills needed for sustainable community development. The project is divided into three main components:
items: Scholarships for undergraduate students
Projects for developing secondary school students
Projects for developing secondary school mathematics teachers

scholarships.title: 1. Undergraduate Scholarships (B.Sc. Scholarships)
scholarships.desc: This first sub-project focuses on cultivating high-quality mathematics graduates to meet community needs. Scholarships are awarded to Thai and Cambodian students, all of whom will receive continuous support to develop their mathematical potential to prepare them for becoming mathematics teachers in Islamic schools in Southern Thailand, or returning to contribute to Cambodia's development.

student.title: 2. High School Student Development (Student Improve)
student.desc: This sub-project aims to elevate the mathematical abilities of high school students in Islamic private schools in Yala, Pattani, and Narathiwat provinces to prepare them with higher capabilities for their future academic paths.

teacher.title: 3. Mathematics Teacher Training (Teacher Improve)
teacher.desc: This final sub-project is crucial for improving the quality of mathematics teaching in private Islamic schools, focusing on reinforcing teachers' knowledge and active pedagogy skills to enable modern, effective classrooms.

mgss.title: About the Mathematical Graduated Students Supporting (MGSS) Project
mgss.sub: Mathematical Graduated Students Supporting Project : MGSS
mgss.desc: This project focuses on supporting scholarships for graduate students in mathematics, and promoting and developing research, teaching, and learning in mathematics and applied mathematics. Funded by the PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund for the project period 2021-2025 (2564-2568 BE), it assists high-potential Thai and international students interested in conducting advanced research and continuing their graduate studies in mathematics, statistics, and applied mathematics. Students enroll in graduate programs at the Department of Mathematics, Faculty of Science, Prince of Songkla University, Hat Yai Campus, and the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus.
mgss.stats_desc: Since 2021, 14 students have been selected to receive the PSU-TUYF scholarship, currently pursuing their master's and doctoral degrees at the Pattani and Hat Yai campuses. Today, the university offers three mathematics graduate curricula:
items: Doctor of Philosophy Program in Mathematics (Hat Yai Campus)
Master of Science Program in Mathematics (Hat Yai Campus)
Master of Science Program in Applied Mathematics and Mathematics Teaching Innovation (Pattani Campus)
mgss.future_desc: Furthermore, the project aims to produce highly capable mathematicians who will serve as key forces in driving the sustainable development of the country.

algebra.title: Algebra Enrichment Project
algebra.sub: โครงการเสริมสร้างความเข้มแข็งทางพีชคณิต
algebra.desc: This project aims to promote and develop algebra education. Supported by the PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund, the PSU-TUYF Algebra Enrichment Project (2024–2028) seeks to enhance knowledge and skills in algebra within Thailand and establish an Algebra Center in the southern region as a key hub for learning and research. The project consists of four sub-projects:
items: Algebra Center: Established to be a center for knowledge exchange and to promote a deeper understanding of algebra.
Algebra Scholarship: Provides scholarships to highly skilled students in algebra.
Algebra Camps: Organizes short-term camps to impart foundational knowledge and advanced concepts in algebra to secondary school students, covering topics like Set Theory, Logic, and Number Theory.
Exchange Staff: Provides funding for Prince of Songkla University personnel to attend language training, study tours, and conduct research in algebra abroad.

footer.dept: Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University.
footer.campus: Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus

hero.title: B.Sc. Scholarships
hero.subtitle: (Undergraduate)
hero.desc: Committed to cultivating high-quality mathematicians to meet societal needs by awarding scholarships to Thai and Cambodian undergraduate students for continuous capacity building.

overview.title: About B.Sc. Scholarships Program
overview.desc1: The PSU-TUYF Scholarship Program is committed to cultivating high-quality mathematicians to meet societal needs by awarding scholarships to Thai and Cambodian undergraduate students. All scholarship recipients will receive continuous mathematical capacity building to prepare them for the future, whether becoming mathematics teachers in Islamic schools in Southern Thailand, or returning to contribute to the development of Cambodia.
overview.desc2: This project commenced in 2021 with support from the PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund and will run until 2025. Currently, 12 students have been selected to receive this scholarship. The project continues to strive to build capable mathematicians to be a crucial driving force for the future development of both nations.

statistics.title: Scholarship Student Statistics
statistics.desc: Statistical report of B.Sc. Scholarships program participants
statistics.alt2: Number of Scholarship Recipients by Nationality
statistics.alt3: Number of Scholarship Recipients by Academic Year

timeline.title: Application Timeline
timeline.desc: Key phases of the application, review, and selection process.

step1.title: Application & Document Submission
step1.desc: Submit the scholarship application form along with latest academic transcripts and personal profile.

step2.title: Interview & Review Phase
step2.desc: List of qualified candidates announced, followed by interviews with the project selection committee.

step3.title: Final Announcement & Orientation
step3.desc: Announcement of scholarship recipients followed by the annual scholarship orientation.

eligibility.title: Applicant Eligibility Criteria
eligibility.desc: Applicants must meet the following criteria to be eligible for the scholarship

items.item1: Applied for Faculty of Science and Technology admission in TCAS Round 1 (1/1 or 1/2 Portfolio) for Mathematics and Computer Science through one of the following projects:
items.item2: Currently in Grade 12 (M.6) or graduated from an Islamic private school in the three southern border provinces (Pattani, Yala, Narathiwat).
items.item3: Cumulative GPAX of at least 3.00, and a 5-semester average grade in Mathematics of at least 2.50.
items.item4: In good physical and mental health, with no contagious or severe illnesses that obstruct studies.
items.item5: Possess a passion for mathematics and a positive attitude towards education-related careers.
items.item6: Responsible, possessing leadership qualities, local consciousness, and a desire to develop their hometown or community in the three southern border provinces.
items.item7: Willing to comply with all terms and conditions set by the program.

subItems1: Special Admission Project for Faculty of Science and Technology
Southern Association of Private Schools Confederation Project
High Academic Achievement Student Admission Project (PSU)
Moral, Ethics, and Public Service Promotion Admission Project

obligations.title: Obligations & Conditions for Recipients
obligations.desc: Recipient conditions and requirements for students under the PSU-TUYF scholarship program
obligations.preamble: Selected students must be eligible to study in the B.Sc. Mathematics & Computer Science program, Faculty of Science and Technology, PSU Pattani Campus, sign the agreement, and comply with these rules:

items.item1: Show diligence in studies and good conduct. In each semester, recipients must submit academic results and conduct certifications from their advisor, program chair, and department head according to the form specified by the scholarship project.
items.item2: Register according to the program curriculum plan. Any deviation must be approved by the project committee. Academic performance must meet the following criteria:

subItems2: GPA in each semester not less than 2.50

hero.title: Student Improvement
hero.subtitle: under PSU-TUYF Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development
hero.desc: Elevating mathematical knowledge and capabilities for high school students in Islamic private schools to strengthen preparedness and academic opportunities in higher education.

overview.title: About Student Improvement Project
overview.desc1: The "Student Improvement" project is part of the larger initiative: PSU-TUYF Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development. It is run by the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus, with support from the PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund. This 5-year project (initial phase 2021-2025) primarily targets students in private Islamic schools in the Southern Border Provinces (Yala, Pattani, Narathiwat) to elevate their mathematical capabilities and prepare them for their future education.
overview.desc2: The subprojects under "Student Improvement" consist of two main parts. First, the Intensive Training Program for Mathematical Excellence (Excellence Math Camp), formerly known as "Pre-Olympics", aims to develop advanced mathematical capabilities of high school students. It prepares them for national and international competitions while strengthening their mathematical processing skills. During 2023-2024, the project focused on preparation for the Mathematical Olympiad, and starting from 2025 onwards, it has expanded its scope to support various other mathematics competition stages.
overview.desc3: Another program is the Mobile Mathematics Project (Math Roadshow), which focuses specifically on preparing Grade 12 students in private Islamic schools in the Southern Border Provinces for higher education. Both subprojects reflect PSU-TUYF's commitment to creating educational opportunities and sustainably developing the potential of youth in the region.

activities.desc: Atmosphere and details of academic capacity building camps and creative thinking workshops.

camp1.title: Math Camp Generation 1
camp1.desc: Intensive high school mathematics tutoring sessions paired with mathematics recreational games to foster positive attitudes.

camp2.title: TCAS Preparation & Guidance Workshop
camp2.desc: Analyzing applied mathematics examination papers and guiding students through science and engineering admissions paths.

eligibility.title: Participant Eligibility Criteria
eligibility.desc: Basic eligibility details for secondary school students seeking to join the capacity building camps.

items.item1: Currently studying in high school (Grade 10 - 12 / M.4 - M.6) in the current academic year.
items.item2: Enrolled in an Islamic private school in the three southern border provinces (Pattani, Yala, Narathiwat).
items.item3: Studying in the Science-Mathematics track or a curriculum focused on science and technology.
items.item4: Passionate about learning mathematics and maintaining good code of conduct.
items.item5: Recommended or certified by a mathematics teacher from their school.

benefits.desc: Key benefits and competencies cultivated through the camp activities.

academic.title: Intensive Academic Knowledge
academic.desc: Acquire deeper understanding of high school mathematics, fast calculations, and logical thinking techniques.

portfolio.title: Camp Participation Certificate
portfolio.desc: Students who complete the camp and pass evaluations will receive certificates to enrich their TCAS Portfolio.

networking.title: Peer Networks & Friendship
networking.desc: Build strong connections with students from other schools in the southern region to exchange knowledge and experiences.

materials.title: Free Materials & Tools
materials.desc: Receive mathematics summary booklets, exam sheets, and activity toolkits free of charge.

hero.title: Teacher Improvement
hero.subtitle: under PSU-TUYF Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development
hero.desc: Promoting skills enhancement and the adoption of pedagogical innovations in mathematics for high school teachers in Islamic private schools.

overview.title: About Teacher Improvement Project
overview.desc1: Prince of Songkla University has received funding support from the TUYF Charitable Trust Fund, Hong Kong Special Administrative Region, People's Republic of China, to support academic and research activities in the form of research grants, scholarships, and academic collaboration with various agencies or organizations in Asia. The Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development project, which is a sub-project under PSU-TUYF operated by the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, has been approved for funding to organize a training project on mathematics instruction and pedagogy for teachers in private Islamic schools under the Office of the Private Education Commission in the three southern border provinces (Pattani, Yala, and Narathiwat) to enhance the teaching capabilities of mathematics teachers. Consequently, the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science is opening applications for mathematics teachers to participate in this training.

activities.title: Teacher Training Courses & Activities
activities.desc: Annual professional development curriculum and workshop schedule for mathematics teachers.

course1.title: Active Learning in Mathematics Classrooms
course1.desc: Focusing on classroom design through group activities, math board games, and GSP or GeoGebra tools.

course2.title: Classroom Action Research & Modern Evaluations
course2.desc: Leveraging PLC processes to solve mathematics student learning gaps, combined with digital evaluation tools.

eligibility.title: Trainee Eligibility Criteria
eligibility.desc: Basic requirements for school representative teachers wishing to participate in the training workshops.

items.item1: Currently teaching mathematics at high school level (Grade 10 - 12 / M.4 - M.6).
items.item2: Affiliated with an Islamic private school in the target provinces (Pattani, Yala, Narathiwat).
items.item3: Committed to improving classroom quality and able to attend the entire duration of the courses.
items.item4: Willing to create new learning resources and implement them in their home school.

benefits.title: Support & Benefits for Teachers
benefits.desc: Benefits, allowances, and tools provided to teachers and participating schools.

certificate.title: Training Certificate
certificate.desc: Teachers who complete the required training hours will receive professional certificates of achievement.

materials.title: Ready-to-use Toolkits & Manuals
materials.desc: Includes lesson plans, hand-made educational games, and evaluation tools for mathematics class.

allowance.title: Travel & Living Allowances
allowance.desc: Travel expenses and allowances supported for teachers traveling from rural or remote districts.

network.title: Professional Mathematics Network
network.desc: Dedicated channels to consult, share exam guides, and support peer teachers within the region.

announcements.desc: Official invitation letters, detailed schedules, and accepted delegate lists for the teacher training courses.

hero.title: Southern Algebra Center
hero.subtitle: Algebra Center
hero.desc: Established to be a center for knowledge exchange and to promote a deeper understanding of algebra.

overview.title: Algebra Center
overview.desc: The Algebra Center is an organization under the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology. It is established to support and develop knowledge in algebra, and to promote instruction and research in related fields.

objectives.title: Objectives
objectives.desc: Objectives of establishing the Algebra Center to support and develop research, as well as create academic cooperation networks.
items: To promote and develop research in algebra, both theoretical and applied.
To develop academic cooperation networks at national and international levels.
To organize training, seminars, and academic activities for instructors, students, and teachers at various levels.
To provide academic services to the community and society on algebra-related topics.

missions.title: Core Mission
missions.desc: Core missions of the Algebra Center in research, personnel development, and promoting academic activities at national and international levels.
items: Conduct research and develop knowledge in algebra, such as Abstract Algebra, Semigroups, Ring theory, etc.
Develop personnel in algebra.
Promote research publication in national and international journals.
Organize regional, national, and international academic conferences/seminars.

personnel.title: Personnel
personnel.desc: Executive committee, working group, and personnel driving the research and operations of the Algebra Center.

items.desc: Responsible for overall administration and oversight.
Supports administration, documentation, and various activities.

activities.title: Key Activities
activities.desc: Seminars, academic conferences, and activities promoting the exchange of algebra knowledge for the academic community.
items: Organizing regional, national, and international academic conferences in algebra.
Organizing workshops for school teachers and university instructors.
Publishing journals or newsletters of the center.
Building collaborative research networks with scholars domestically and abroad.

announcements.title: Announcements & Publications
announcements.desc: Follow news, seminar announcements, and official notices from the Southern Algebra Center.

hero.title: Undergraduate Scholarships in Algebra
hero.subtitle: Algebra Scholarships
hero.desc: Provides scholarships to highly skilled students in algebra.

overview.title: Algebra Scholarships
overview.desc: Algebra Scholarships under the PSU-TUYF Algebra Enrichment Project aim to select and continuously support undergraduate students with outstanding academic records and mathematical talents in algebra to foster high-potential scholars and new researchers.

objectives.title: Objectives
objectives.desc: Objectives of granting algebra scholarships to develop high-potential new researchers.
items: To support and develop students with outstanding mathematical abilities, especially in algebra, to continue their studies at a higher level and become high-potential new researchers.

eligibility.title: Eligibility Criteria
eligibility.desc: Basic qualifications and academic criteria for algebra scholarship applicants.
items: Graduated from high school or currently studying in Grade 12 (Science-Math or related program).
Minimum Cumulative Grade Point Average (GPAX) of 3.25.
Minimum grade average of 3.50 in Mathematics and English.

Demonstrated excellent and evident mathematical skills, e.g., winning a first prize in regional/national math competitions / passing at least Camp 2 of POSN in Mathematics / completing a mathematics project presented at an academic conference and receiving a presentation award of 'Good' level or higher, etc.

details.title: Scholarship Details
details.desc: Funding rates, tuition support, living allowance, and exchange research travel grants.

items.title: Tuition Fees
items.desc: Covers full tuition fees throughout the 4-year undergraduate study period.

items.title: Research Exchange Grant
items.desc: Travel and living expenses support for a 1-semester study abroad exchange, worth 250,000 THB.

items.title: Skill Development Grant
items.desc: Grant for presenting research at mathematics conferences, worth 15,000 THB.

obligations.title: Obligations of Scholars
obligations.desc: Academic requirements, course registrations, and research presentations during the scholarship.
items: Maintain a Cumulative Grade Point Average (GPAX) of at least 3.25 at the end of each academic year.

Register for algebra-related courses (e.g., Principle of Mathematics, Set Theory, Semigroup Theory, Abstract Algebra, Linear Algebra, Group Theory, or Ring Theory) for at least 15 credits, earning a grade of B+ or higher in these courses.
Conduct an algebra research project under the supervision of a center advisor.
Present research at a national or international mathematics conference at least once before graduation.
Study or conduct research in algebra as an exchange student abroad for 1 semester during the 3rd or 4th year.
Must graduate within 4 academic years.

curriculum.title: Eligible Curriculums
curriculum.desc: Undergraduate mathematics programs that support the PSU-TUYF Algebra Scholarships.
items: B.Sc. in Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus.
B.Sc. in Mathematics, Faculty of Science, Prince of Songkla University, Hat Yai Campus.

hero.title: Algebra Enrichment Camps
hero.subtitle: Algebra Camps
hero.desc: Organizes short-term camps to transmit basic knowledge and advanced concepts in algebra to high school students, such as set theory, mathematical logic, and number theory.

overview.title: Algebra Camps
overview.desc: Algebra Camps are short-term camps organized to provide deep knowledge and inspire junior and senior high school students with a special interest in algebra, aiming to attract highly interested students and strengthen their foundation in core algebra concepts.

target.title: Target Group
target.desc: Main groups of trainees whom the project aims to support and develop in algebraic skills.
items: Secondary school students with an interest in mathematics, especially in algebra.
Mathematical Olympiad students (Camp 1 and Camp 2) from all centers nationwide.

details.title: Camp Details
details.desc: Format, scale of the short-term training camp, and core subjects trained during the camp.
items: Short-term algebra camp for 20 selected students.
Training in fundamental algebra topics, such as set theory, mathematical logic, and number theory.

eligibility.title: Applicant Eligibility
eligibility.desc: Selection criteria and portfolio documents required for camp application.
eligibility.subDesc: Interested students must submit their portfolios through Google Forms, which should contain the following details:
items: First name - Last name, age, and educational institution.
Extra-curricular and academic activities performed during studies.
Awards or certificates received related to mathematics.
Statement of purpose and motivation for joining the Algebra Camp.
eligibility.note: Note: The camp committee will evaluate the portfolios, and all applicants will undergo an interview for final selection.

hero.title: Staff Exchange & Research Grants
hero.subtitle: Exchange Staff
hero.desc: Provides funding for PSU staff to learn languages, observe study, and conduct algebra research abroad.

overview.title: Staff Exchange & Research Grants (Exchange Staff)
overview.desc: This project aims to provide opportunities for faculty and staff members of Prince of Songkla University (PSU), Pattani and Hat Yai campuses, to gain international experience and enhance their algebra knowledge through study observation and research at leading global academic institutions.

activities.title: Project Activities
activities.desc: Staff exchange activities, teaching observation, and overseas study and research grants.

items.title: Algebra Teaching Observation Abroad
items.desc: Staff members will visit and observe algebra teaching methodologies in secondary schools and universities abroad, such as in Hong Kong, China, USA, Europe, and Asia, to adapt modern knowledge and techniques for education development in Thailand.

items.title: Overseas Study and Research Grants for PSU Staff
items.desc: Offers opportunities for staff from the Faculty of Science and Technology (PSU Pattani Campus) and the Faculty of Science (PSU Hat Yai Campus) to travel for algebra study or research for one academic semester in various Asian and European countries (e.g., Hong Kong, China, USA, Europe, Asia) to build academic networks and expertise.

requirements.title: Conditions & Notes
requirements.desc: Specific conditions and important instructions for staff traveling abroad for study and research.
requirements.note: Note: Staff intending to conduct research in China must successfully complete Chinese language courses before traveling.

algebraEnrichment.intro: The PSU-TUYF Algebra Enrichment Project (2024–2028) is operated by the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University, with the support of the PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund. It aims to elevate knowledge and capabilities in algebra within Thailand, and establish an Algebra Center in the southern region as a key hub for learning and research.

advisor.title: Project Advisory Committee
advisor.desc: This project receives guidance and strategic support from an advisory committee consisting of leading professors and experts from both domestic and international institutions, working together to guide direction, approve grants, and monitor project progress to ensure the highest quality and efficiency.

rationale.title: Rationale & Background
rationale.desc: Algebra is the cornerstone of analytical thinking, systematic problem-solving, and innovation across fields like science, engineering, computer science, economics, and more. Understanding algebra not only builds necessary skills for advanced studies but also serves as a critical tool to evaluate data, predict outcomes, and solve complex real-world problems. This project was established to create opportunities and fulfill the advanced mathematical potential of Thai youth.

objectives.title: Project Objectives
items: Establish an knowledge exchange center and promote deep understanding in algebra.
Provide scholarships to undergraduate students with outstanding capabilities in algebra.
Organize short-term camps to impart foundational knowledge and advanced concepts in algebra to high school students, covering topics like Set Theory, Logic, and Number Theory.
Support Prince of Songkla University personnel with funding for language training, study tours, and algebra research abroad.

items.title: Elevating Algebra Research
items.desc: To establish networks between domestic and international researchers.

items.title: Producing Specialized Graduates
items.desc: Students can conduct algebra research under close academic supervision.

items.title: Enhancing Intensive Algebra Knowledge
items.desc: Specifically provided to secondary school students.

items.title: Building a Strong Academic Community
items.desc: Creating a dedicated space for exchanging research and expertise among algebra scholars.

center.objective: Establish a knowledge exchange center and promote deep understanding in algebra.
center.rationale: The Southern Algebra Center was established to support advanced mathematics knowledge exchange, providing a space for sharing research and building academic networks to elevate Thai mathematics capabilities globally.

scholarships.objective: Provide scholarships to undergraduate students with outstanding capabilities in algebra.
scholarships.rationale: Focuses on selecting and providing continuous funding to undergraduate students with outstanding academic results and skills in algebra, nurturing next-generation high-potential researchers.

camps.objective: Organize short-term camps to impart foundational knowledge and advanced concepts in algebra to high school students, covering topics like Set Theory, Logic, and Number Theory.
camps.rationale: The Algebra Enrichment Camps aim to inspire and deliver advanced theoretical topics in a concise and understandable format, laying the deep academic foundations required for secondary school students.

exchange.objective: Support Prince of Songkla University personnel with funding for language training, study tours, and algebra research abroad.
exchange.rationale: Elevate research standards and academic staff capabilities through international exchange programs, promoting language learning, academic tours, and collaborative world-class research.

outcomes.title: The PSU-TUYF Algebra Enrichment Project consists of 4 sub-projects

items.title: Southern Algebra Center
items.desc: Established to be the first algebra hub in Southern Thailand, providing advice, consultation, and promoting research collaboration in algebra.

items.title: Undergraduate Scholarships
items.desc: Awards scholarships to high-potential high school students to pursue undergraduate studies in mathematics with an emphasis on algebra at Prince of Songkla University (Pattani and Hat Yai campuses), along with supporting research presentations and overseas research opportunities.

items.title: Algebra Enrichment Camps
items.desc: Organizes intensive short-term camps for secondary school students with a special interest in mathematics, particularly algebra, to reinforce their knowledge and deep conceptual understanding in algebra.

items.title: Exchange Staff & Overseas Research Grants
items.desc: Supports Prince of Songkla University personnel to study tours or conduct research in algebra for one semester at leading universities abroad, such as China, USA, Europe, etc., and promotes necessary language acquisition.

hero.title: PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund
hero.subtitle: กองทุน PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund
hero.desc: Fund supporting Prince of Songkla University academic and research activities.

history.title: Background
history.desc: Prince of Songkla University has received funding support from the TUYF Charitable Trust Fund, Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of the People's Republic of China, to support academic and research activities in the form of research grants and scholarships. A total of 4 projects have been supported over a period of 5 years (the first phase 2021-2025, totaling 55,622,276 Baht). To ensure the efficient operation of the fund, Prince of Songkla University has appointed the executive committee of the PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund and signed a Donation Agreement with HSBC Trustee (Hong Kong) Limited, which is the governing body of the TUYF Charitable Trust Fund in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of the People's Republic of China.

objectives.title: Fund Objectives
objectives.desc: Objectives of the fund to support human resource development and research in the fields of oceanography, mathematics, and epidemiology in Asia.
items: Support human resource development in oceanography, mathematics, and epidemiology in Asia through international curricula / training programs of Prince of Songkla University.
Promote and strengthen research activities in the three aforementioned disciplines.
Strengthen scientific networks of the three disciplines in Asia, with Prince of Songkla University serving as the coordinating center.
Support local and international communities in living lives connected to science in the three disciplines above.

hero.title: Excellence Math Camp
hero.subtitle: Intensive Training Program for Mathematical Excellence under the support of the TUYF Charitable Trust Fund

overview.title: Intensive Training Program for Mathematical Excellence (Excellence Math Camp)
overview.desc1: The Intensive Training Program for Mathematical Excellence (Excellence Math Camp), formerly known as "Pre-Olympics", focuses on developing advanced mathematical capabilities for high school students. It prepares them for national and international mathematics competitions while strengthening their mathematical processing skills. During 2023-2024, the project focused primarily on preparing students for the Mathematical Olympiad stage. From 2025 onwards, the project has expanded its scope to support various other mathematics competition stages.
overview.desc2: Participants will receive intensive tutoring from experts, practice solving complex and diverse problems, and build networks of students with similar interests in mathematics. This program is free of charge, supporting lunch, dinner, snacks, and accommodation throughout the 10 days of training to allow students to focus fully on learning and self-development.

hero.title: Math Roadshow
hero.subtitle: Mobile Mathematics Project under the support of the TUYF Charitable Trust Fund

overview.title: Mobile Mathematics Project (Math Roadshow)
overview.desc1: The Mobile Mathematics Project (Math Roadshow) is operated by the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus, supported by the PSU-TUYF Charitable Trust Fund. It is committed to elevating mathematics education for students in private Islamic schools in the Southern Border Provinces (Pattani, Yala, and Narathiwat).
overview.desc2: These private Islamic schools play a vital role in educating Muslim youth, often integrating religious studies into the general curriculum. However, a key challenge is the shortage of specialized mathematics teachers, which prevents students from fully developing their mathematical potential.
overview.desc3: Prince of Songkla University recognizes this necessity and has established the Mobile Mathematics Project (Math Roadshow). The project aims to reduce educational gaps by directly sending mathematics experts from Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus, to organize training workshops and provide mathematics tutorial sessions for students directly at schools.
overview.obj1: To promote and develop students in private Islamic schools to acquire mathematical process skills and positive attitudes towards mathematics.
overview.obj2: To improve mathematics academic achievements of students in private Islamic schools.
overview.obj3: This project will help strengthen mathematical capabilities for students, opening opportunities for them to develop knowledge and abilities continuously and to their full potential in the future.

pom2023.title: Intensive Training Project for POSN Exam Preparation in Mathematics (PSU-TUYF Pre-Olympic Math) 2023
pom2023.subtitle: Under PSU-TUYF: Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development
pom2023.desc1: The "Pre Olympics" Mathematics Project (PSU-TUYF: Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development 2023) is organized by the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus. This project stems from the recognition of the importance of promoting Thai youth with high potential in science and mathematics, particularly in the Southern Border Provinces. This aligns with the mission of the Promotion of Academic Olympiads and Development of Science Education Foundation (POSN) to develop high school students' potential to be ready as Thailand's representatives in international Olympiad competitions.
pom2023.desc2: The primary problem this project addresses is that students in private Islamic schools in the three southern border provinces (the main target group) have limited opportunities to access additional mathematics training for POSN selection exams. Furthermore, these schools lack mathematics teachers specialized in Olympiad mathematics, resulting in limited success rate and POSN Camp 1 participation. This situation restricts the potential development of youth in this region.
pom2023.desc3: Consequently, the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science recognizes the necessity of organizing training to prepare students for the POSN selection exam. Supported by the Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development project under PSU-TUYF, this initiative will increase opportunities for students in private Islamic schools in the Southern Border Provinces to fully develop their mathematical abilities and skills, leading to an increase in the number of students passing and participating in the POSN academic Olympiad project in the future.
objectivesList: To prepare students with mathematical potential for the selection examination into Camp 1 of the POSN Academic Olympiad Foundation (Mathematics Division).
To promote and develop students in the three southern border provinces to have enhanced mathematical process skills.

scheduleList.detail: Announcement of qualified students (80 students and 40 reserves)
Deadline for enrollment confirmation (for qualified students)
Announcement of reserve list (if any vacancy occurs)

pom2024.title: Intensive Training Project for POSN Exam Preparation in Mathematics (PSU-TUYF Pre-Olympic Math) 2024
pom2024.subtitle: Under PSU-TUYF: Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development
pom2024.desc1: The Intensive Training Project for POSN Exam Preparation in Mathematics (PSU-TUYF Pre-Olympic Math) 2024 is organized by the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus.
pom2024.desc2: This initiative serves as a continuous project, organized for the second consecutive year to prepare students with mathematical potential in the three Southern Border Provinces (Yala, Pattani, Narathiwat), especially those from private Islamic schools. It aims to equip them with the necessary knowledge and skills for the POSN Academic Olympiad Camp 1 selection examination (Mathematics Division), while strengthening mathematical process skills of local youth.
pom2024.desc3: Recognizing the importance of providing youth in the region with opportunities equal to those nationwide, particularly in mathematics—which forms the foundation of analytical thinking and complex problem-solving—the project this year has been enhanced and optimized based on the excellent response from 2023. Participants will receive intensive tutoring on key mathematics exam contents from Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus faculty members over 10 days. The project covers all expenses, including lunch, dinner, snacks, and accommodation throughout the training period.
objectivesList: To prepare students with mathematical potential for the selection examination into Camp 1 of the POSN Academic Olympiad Foundation (Mathematics Division).
To promote and develop students in the three southern border provinces to have enhanced mathematical process skills.

scheduleList.detail: Announcement of qualified students (40 students and 10 reserves)
Deadline for enrollment confirmation (for qualified students)
Announcement of reserve list (if any vacancy occurs)

emc2025.title: Intensive Training Project for Excellence in Mathematics (Excellence Math Camp) 2025
emc2025.subtitle: Under PSU-TUYF: Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development
emc2025.desc1: The Intensive Training Project for Excellence in Mathematics (Excellence Math Camp) under the concept "PSU-TUYF: Mathematics for Sustainable Community Development" is organized by the Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science and Technology, Prince of Songkla University, Pattani Campus.
emc2025.desc2: This project focuses on developing advanced mathematical potential for students in private Islamic schools in the three Southern Border Provinces (Yala, Pattani, Narathiwat) who are entering grades 8 - 11 (M.2 - M.5) in the academic year 2025, to prepare them for both national and international mathematics competitions and strengthen mathematical process skills.
emc2025.desc3: Participants will receive intensive tutoring from experts, practice complex and diverse problems, and build a network of peer students sharing the same interest in mathematics. The project is free of charge, supporting lunch, dinner, snacks, and accommodation throughout the 10 days of training.
objectivesList: To prepare students with mathematical potential for participating in national and international mathematics competitions.
To promote and develop students in the three southern border provinces to have enhanced mathematical process skills.

scheduleList.detail: Announcement of qualified students (40 students and 10 reserves)
Deadline for enrollment confirmation (for qualified students)
Announcement of qualified students in reserve list (if any vacancy occurs)
